name: Thiabendazole
description: An anthelmintic drug designed to inhibit the helminth-specific enzyme fumarate reductase, preventing cell glucose uptake in intestinal nematodes.
preparation: Oral tablets: 25mg/kg twice daily after fat-rich meals, administered for 2-7 days depending on clinical parasite density.
safetyAlert: Frequently causes transient nausea, vomiting, dizziness, and mild liver enzyme elevations.
interactions: Increases plasma concentration of active drugs like Theophylline.
